name: Grilled Shrimp Tacos
description: These grilled shrimp tacos are seasoned with lime juice, garlic and cumin and served in corn tortillas with a creamy cabbage slaw, corn and cotija cheese.
recipeIngredient: 1 pound large raw shrimp (peeled and deveined)
1 tablespoon tequila ((I used an aged añejo tequila for a smoky flavor, but any kind will work))
1 tablespoon lime juice ((about juice of 1/2 lime))
4 cups thinly sliced cabbage ((green, red or a combination of both))
2 tablespoons lime juice ((about juice of 1 lime))
recipeInstructions: Prepare grill: Preheat grill to medium-high heat (about 400°F-450°F) for direct-heat grilling. While grill is heating up, make the slaw.(For gas grilling, simply light all the burners on high, close the lid and let the grill heat up for 5-10 minutes. For charcoal grilling, the process is a bit more involved. Here's a super-quick read showing you step-by-step how to start a charcoal grill.)

Make the slaw: Add all slaw ingredients to a medium bowl and toss to combine. Taste and season with more salt if desired. If you prefer your slaw a little more dressed, add in more mayo and/or sour cream. Refrigerate until ready to use.

Grill the corn: Grill the ears of corn with the lid closed for 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until corn is caramelized and dark brown spots appear on the kernels. Transfer to a large plate or baking sheet and set aside to cool for 5 minutes.
Slice corn: When cool enough to handle, slice corn kernels off the cob and into a bowl. Set aside.
Season shrimp: In a medium bowl, combine all the ingredients for the shrimp. Toss together until evenly coated.

Skewer: Thread the shrimp onto 4-6 metal skewers. (If using wooden skewers, be sure to soak them in water for 15 minutes before threading shrimp to help prevent the skewers from burning on the grill.)
Grill: Grill the shrimp for 4-5 minutes, turning halfway through cooking, or until the shrimp are firm to the touch and opaque at the center.
Assemble tacos: Remove the shrimp from the skewers and serve in warm corn tortillas with slaw, grilled corn, cotija cheese and a spritz of fresh lime.

ai.cliffNotes: Season shrimp heavily with cumin, chili powder, and lime, then grill quickly over high heat until just opaque to keep them tender while developing char, then balance with a cool creamy slaw and charred corn.
keyTips: Preheat grill to 400-450°F and grill shrimp only 4-5 minutes total, turning once—overcooked shrimp becomes rubbery and tough
Thread shrimp onto skewers and soak wooden skewers 15 minutes before grilling to prevent burning and ensure even cooking
Make the slaw ahead and refrigerate—the cool, creamy cabbage provides essential textural and temperature contrast to hot grilled components
Char the corn with grill lid closed for 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until dark brown spots appear for caramelized sweetness that complements the spiced shrimp
